title: Code Factory: Proof-by-Sabotage Software Factory
description:
Code Factory is a local-first, open-source workflow for turning specifications, behavior checks, design-token contracts, policy verification, and release receipts into auditable software delivery. Version 0.17 completes Product Missions and the signed Capability Pack system: a deterministic PRD-to-Product-Graph compiler, exact dependency-ordered value slices, supervised Loop Passport missions, independent no-finish verification, evidence-linked PR drafts, classified outcomes, Meter v2 telemetry, and 29 signed packs. Seven executable starter targets cover CLI, API, MCP, worker, web, Expo mobile, and supervised agent UI software. Composable surface, language, capability, data, and operations packs add explicit compatibility contracts without pretending product-specific integrations already exist. Every pack binds its file map to an offline DSSE Ed25519 signature and must reject ten meaningful structural mutations before installation or composition. Factory Studio, VS Code, and JetBrains IDEs expose the same bounded workflow. This source release includes public documentation assets, color Mermaid diagrams, exact Studio captures, the narrated Remotion quick start built from the shipped UI, and a nine-stage conceptual visual walkthrough. The concept illustrations are not UI screenshots or measured outcome evidence.

Evidence boundary: environmental content is accepted only as explicitly supplied untrusted local data. This release does not scrape, poll, schedule, authenticate connectors, invoke models, execute agents, or grant merge, deployment, publication, signing, credential, connector, or external-message authority. Capability Pack validation, installation, and composition grant no runtime or release authority. Safe auto-resolution cannot invent product facts or approve external effects. Outcome records preserve measured, observed, modeled, and unknown evidence classes. A passing receipt does not prove an arbitrary production environment.

Dual licensed under MIT or Apache-2.0.
